question.text: The phenomenon of light bouncing back into the same medium from a surface is ____.
According to laws of reflection, the angle of incidence is ____ to the angle of reflection.
question.explanation: i = r for all smooth surfaces.

question.text: A virtual image is one which ____ be caught on a screen.
question.explanation: Formed by diverging rays that appear to meet.

question.text: The focal length of a plane mirror is ____.
question.explanation: Planes mirror has no curvature (R = infinity).

question.text: The magnification produced by a plane mirror is ____.
question.explanation: Image is same size and erect.

question.text: Relation between radius of curvature R and focal length f of a spherical mirror is ____.
Which mirror is always used as a rear-view mirror in vehicles?
question.explanation: Provides wide field of view and erect image.

question.text: Which mirror is used by dentists to see larger images of teeth?
question.explanation: Can produce magnified virtual image when object is close.

question.text: Refractive index of a medium is defined as n = ____.
question.explanation: Ratio of light speed in vacuum to medium.

question.text: Snell's Law is expressed as ____.
question.explanation: Constant is the relative refractive index.

question.text: When light travels from rarer to denser medium, it bends ____ the normal.
The phenomenon responsible for the twinkling of stars is ____.
question.explanation: Fluctuating air density changes refractive index.

question.text: Critical angle is the angle of incidence in denser medium for which r = ____.
Total Internal Reflection (TIR) occurs only when light travels from ____.
question.explanation: Required for r to reach 90 before i.

question.text: Optical fibers work on the principle of ____.
question.explanation: Light stays trapped in the core.

question.text: Mirage is an optical illusion occurring due to ____.
question.explanation: Refraction through air layers of different temperatures.

question.text: Lens Maker's Formula relates f with ____.
Power of a lens P = ____.
Unit of power of a lens is ____.
Total power of combination of lenses in contact is P = ____.
Splitting of white light into constituent colors is called ____.
question.explanation: Occurs due to different speeds of colors in prism.

question.text: Which color of light bends the most through a glass prism?
Rainbow is formed due to a combination of ____, ____ and ____.
question.explanation: Interaction of sunlight with water droplets.

question.text: Blue color of the sky is due to ____ of light.
Red color of Sun during sunrise/sunset is because red light is ____ scattered.
A convex lens behaves as a ____ lens.
A concave lens behaves as a ____ lens.
question.explanation: Spreads rays away from principle axis.

question.text: A simple microscope uses a single ____ lens of short focal length.
A compound microscope has two lenses: ____ and ____.
In an astronomical telescope, the objective has a ____ focal length than eyepiece.
question.explanation: To gather more light and provide high magnification.

question.text: Magnifying power of a simple microscope is M = ____.
question.explanation: When image is at least distance of distinct vision.

question.text: Normal eye has a least distance of distinct vision D = ____ cm.
Myopia (Short-sightedness) is corrected using a ____ lens.
question.explanation: Focuses the image back on retina.

question.text: Hypermetropia (Long-sightedness) is corrected using a ____ lens.
question.explanation: Brings the image forward to retina.

question.text: Astigmatism is corrected using ____ lenses.
Mirror Formula for spherical mirrors is ____.
Lens Formula for thin lenses is ____.
The ratio of height of image to height of object is called ____.
Focal length of a convex lens is ____.
Focal length of a concave lens is ____.
Relationship between u, v and R for refraction at a single spherical surface: ____.
question.explanation: Standard refraction formula for spherical interface.

question.text: Linear magnification of a lens m = ____.
question.explanation: Different from mirror magnification which is -v/u.

question.text: Angle of deviation δ produced by a thin prism of angle A is δ = ____.
Minimum deviation occurs when angle of incidence i is ____ angle of emergence e.
question.explanation: Symmetric path of light through prism.

question.text: Magnifying power of a simple microscope when image is at infinity: M = ____.
Magnifying power of compound microscope when final image is at D: M = ____.
question.explanation: Product of magnifications of objective and eyepiece.

question.text: In a telescope, the length of the tube in normal adjustment is L = ____.
Relationship between critical angle ic and refractive index n is sin ic = ____.
Apparent depth of an object submerged in liquid of refractive index n is h_app = ____.
question.explanation: Bending towards normal makes it look shallower.

question.text: Shift produced by a glass slab of thickness t and refractive index n is ____.
question.explanation: Measure of how much object appears moved.

question.text: Condition for no dispersion but deviation in combination of two prisms is ____.
Magnification of a concave mirror when object is at C is ____.
What happens to focal length of a lens when it is immersed in liquid of nL = nG?
Primary rainbow is formed by ____ TIR and ____ refractions.
Secondary rainbow is formed by ____ TIR and ____ refractions.
question.explanation: Colors are reversed compared to primary.

question.text: For a compound microscope, objective focal length fo is ____ eyepiece focal length fe.
question.explanation: Both are short, but fo < fe.

question.text: A convex mirror always forms a ____ image.
question.explanation: Image is always behind the mirror within F.

question.text: A concave lens always forms a ____ image.
question.explanation: Ray diverge, principle focus is virtual.

question.text: Refractive index of diamond is approx ____.
question.explanation: Very high, leading to small critical angle and brilliance.

question.text: Scattering of light by colloidal particles is called ____.
question.explanation: Size of particles comparable to wavelength.

question.text: If an object is at 2f of convex lens, image is at ____.
Ratio of focal lengths f_red / f_violet for a lens is ____.
Focal length of a lens in air is f. In water it becomes approx ____.
question.explanation: Decrease in relative refractive index increases f.

question.text: Image formed by a concave mirror is virtual only when object is between ____.
question.explanation: Magnified and erect image behind mirror.

question.text: Magnifying power of reflecting telescope (Cassegrain) is M = ____.
Advantage of reflecting telescope is ____.
question.explanation: Mirrors reflect all colors at same angle.

question.text: Refractive index of atmosphere decreases with ____.
question.explanation: Air becomes thinner at higher altitudes.

question.text: Prism formula relating n and A for min deviation δm is n = ____.
question.explanation: Used to measure refractive index of material.

question.text: Resolving power of a microscope is inversely proportional to ____.
Rayleigh's criterion for resolution: θ_min = ____.
Which part of eye acts as a variable aperture?
Retina of the eye acts as a ____.
Accommodation of eye is the ability to change ____ of eye lens.
Smallest angular separation resolved by eye is approx ____.
Angular magnification of telescope M = ____.
question.explanation: Ratio of angles subtended at eye by image and object.

question.text: Velocity of light in glass (n=1.5) is ____ m/s.
For a compound microscope, L = 20cm, fo = 1cm, fe = 5cm. M is approx ____.
Critical angle for glass (n=1.5) into air is approx ____.
Optical path is defined as ____.
question.explanation: Distance light would travel in vacuum in same time.

question.text: Is it possible for a convex lens to act as a diverging lens?
For a prism of angle A, if the refractive index is n = cot(A/2), the angle of minimum deviation is ____.
question.explanation: Using prism formula and n = cos(A/2)/sin(A/2).

question.text: A transparent cube of side L contains a small air bubble. Apparent depth from one face is 6cm, from opposite face is 4cm. If n=1.5, L is ____ cm.
question.explanation: Real depth sum = n * (d1 + d2) = 1.5 * (6 + 4) = 15 cm.

question.text: If focal length of objective is fo and eyepiece is fe, magnifying power of telescope in normal adjustment is M. If they are interchanged, power becomes ____.
In a Cassegrain telescope, the secondary mirror is a ____ mirror.
question.explanation: Reflects light through a hole in the primary parabolic mirror.

question.text: Total deviation produced by two plane mirrors inclined at 60° for a ray incident on one is ____.
A convex lens is silvered at the back. It behaves as a ____ mirror.
question.explanation: Light passes twice and reflects once. Effective power = 2PL + PM.

question.text: If two thin lenses of powers P1 and P2 are separated by distance d, effective power P is ____.
The critical angle for a medium is 45°. Its refractive index is ____.
For a point object placed at distance u from a convex lens of focal length f, image distance is v. Graph of 1/v vs 1/u is a ____.
Focal length of a glass lens is 10cm. For red light, focal length will be ____ than 10cm.
Blue color of sky is seen through a yellow filter as ____.
Rayleigh scattering intensity I ∝ ____.
What type of image is formed on the retina of a human eye?
question.explanation: Brain flips it back to erect.

question.text: Is it possible for a diverging lens to form a real image?
question.explanation: If rays are converging to a point behind lens.

question.text: Standardizing Ray Optics (Chapter 107) complete? (120 qns)
All questions follow 6-line schema.
Explanations provided for all questions?
Focal length of a mirror immersed in water ____.
question.explanation: Depends only on R, not on surrounding medium.

question.text: The ratio of velocities of light of two different frequencies in vacuum is ____.
question.explanation: Universal constant c for all frequencies.

question.text: If angle of prism A=60 and n=√2, angle of minimum deviation is ____.
Angular dispersion produced by a prism is dependent on ____.
question.explanation: Difference in deviations of extreme colors.

question.text: A fish looks up from water (n=4/3) at the sky. It sees the whole world within a cone of angle approx ____.
If eye is moved away from eyepiece of microscope, magnification ____.
question.explanation: Field of view decreases but M is constant.

question.text: Magnifying power of a telescope for distant object is M. For closer object, M ____.
question.explanation: Image moves away from focus, reducing angular magnification.

question.text: Wavefront is a surface of constant ____.
question.explanation: Points on it oscillate in unison.

question.text: Huygen's principle treats every point on wavefront as a source of ____.
question.explanation: New wavefront is the forward envelope of these wavelets.

question.text: Reflection at infinity for a point source gives a ____ wavefront.
Does Ray Optics consider wave nature of light?
The value of n for a medium is 1. The medium is likely ____.
Is it possible to have zero deviation but dispersion?
question.explanation: Combination of prisms with balanced net deviation.

question.text: Intensity of image in telescope depends on ____ of objective.
Chromastic aberration is a defect ____ mirrors.
question.explanation: Mirror reflection is not color dependent.

question.text: Spherical aberration is minimized by using ____.
question.explanation: Eliminates separate focal points for marginal rays.

question.text: Unit of refractive index is ____.
A light ray passes from air to glass. Frequency ____.
Total number of reflections in a periscope is ____.
Which phenomenon supports particle nature of light?
question.explanation: Wave theory fails to explain emission threshold.

question.text: Distance between focus and pole is ____.
A real image is always formed by a ____ lens for a real object.
Batch 3 Optics - Chapter 1 (Ray Optics) Standardization complete.
